title: Python Foundations
description: Part A of the AI Native Engineering programme. The programming baseline for the whole programme, delivered entirely in Google Colab. Covers Modules I-V of the standard Python syllabus plus Git and professional tooling. 30 hours, Weeks 1-8.

modules.title: P1 . Module I . Introduction
modules.description: The on-ramp for learners who know how to use a computer but have never programmed. Everything runs in Google Colab in the browser, no installation needed. Confident, correct use of Python's most basic building blocks: variables, types, operators, and output.
modules.tooltip: Week 1 . 4 hrs . Colab, variables, types, operators, conversion, f-strings, type hints, match-case

topics.title: Unit 1.1 - The Python Environment
topics.tooltip: Why Python for AI work, the interpreter, Google Colab, running your first program

topics.title: Unit 1.2 - Variables, Identifiers & Types
topics.tooltip: Variables and assignment, identifiers, values and types, inspecting types

topics.title: Unit 1.3 - Operators & Expressions
topics.tooltip: Arithmetic, precedence, comparison, logical operators, truthiness

topics.title: Unit 1.4 - Statements, Conversion & Output
topics.tooltip: Statements, type conversion, string basics, f-strings, type hints, match-case, comments

modules.title: P2 . Module II . Control Structures, Functions & Tooling
modules.description: Learners move from single statements to programs that make decisions, repeat work, and are organised into reusable functions and modules. The module closes with the professional tooling - virtual environments, pip, Poetry, and Pytest - that every later module assumes.
modules.tooltip: Weeks 2-3 . 8 hrs . Builds on P1 - syntax, variables, types, operators, output

topics.title: Unit 2.1 - Conditionals
topics.tooltip: if/elif/else, nested conditionals, boolean expressions in conditions, conditional (ternary) expression

topics.title: Unit 2.2 - Loops
topics.tooltip: while loop, for loop, range(), enumerate() and zip(), break and continue, nested loops

topics.title: Unit 2.3 - Functions
topics.tooltip: Defining and calling functions, parameters, scope, recursion, documentation

topics.title: Unit 2.4 - Functional Constructs
topics.tooltip: Lambda functions, higher-order functions, decorators, generators

topics.title: Unit 2.5 - Modules, Packaging & Professional Tooling
topics.tooltip: Modules and imports, standard library, pip, virtual environments, Poetry, Pytest

modules.title: P3 . Module III . Data Structures
modules.description: The four core collections - lists, tuples, sets, and dictionaries - plus comprehensions, iterators, and the collections module. These are the structures every AI lab in Part B relies on to store prompts, results, scores, and records.
modules.tooltip: Weeks 4-5 . 8 hrs . Builds on P2 - loops, functions, and iteration

topics.title: Unit 3.1 - Lists
topics.tooltip: Creating and indexing, slicing with step, mutability, list methods, sorting, nested lists, list comprehension

topics.title: Unit 3.2 - Tuples
topics.tooltip: Creating and accessing, tuple assignment and unpacking, nested tuples, immutability, basic operations

topics.title: Unit 3.3 - Sets
topics.tooltip: Creating sets, set operations, membership and mutation, set comprehension

topics.title: Unit 3.4 - Dictionaries
topics.tooltip: Creating and accessing, adding/modifying/deleting, looping, sorting, nested dictionaries, built-in functions, dictionary comprehension

topics.title: Unit 3.5 - Iterators, Generators & Collections
topics.tooltip: Iterables vs iterators, generators recap, the collections module (Counter, defaultdict, namedtuple)

modules.title: P4 . Module IV . Classes & Objects
modules.description: Object-oriented thinking: modelling domain concepts as classes with attributes and behaviour, extending them with inheritance, and reducing boilerplate with dataclasses. This is how learners will structure their capstone tools.
modules.tooltip: Week 6 . 4 hrs . Builds on P3 - collections; P2 - functions and scope

topics.title: Unit 4.1 - Object-Oriented Foundations
topics.tooltip: Abstract data types, classes and objects, the constructor, methods

topics.title: Unit 4.2 - The Four Pillars of OOP
topics.tooltip: Encapsulation, abstraction, inheritance (single, multi-level, multiple, MRO), and polymorphism

topics.title: Unit 4.3 - Special Methods & Dataclasses
topics.tooltip: Operator overloading/dunder methods, dataclasses, libraries and packages in OOP

modules.title: P5 . Module V . Files & Exception Handling
modules.description: Reading and writing files (including CSV and JSON) and handling the errors that real data always produces. Culminates in a robust file reader - the pattern behind every data-driven AI tool the learner will build.
modules.tooltip: Week 7 . 4 hrs . Builds on P4 - objects; P3 - collections

topics.title: Unit 5.1 - File Handling
topics.tooltip: Introduction to files, file operations, context managers, reading CSVs, working with JSON

topics.title: Unit 5.2 - Errors & Exceptions
topics.tooltip: Errors vs exceptions, exception handling, handling multiple exceptions, common exceptions, raising exceptions

topics.title: Unit 5.3 - Case Study
topics.tooltip: Building a robust file reader - logging bad rows and continuing, separating valid from invalid data

modules.title: P6 . Git, GitHub & Portfolio
modules.description: The professional habit of version control and the setup of the personal portfolio repository used across the whole programme. Delivered through the GitHub web interface - no command line required - and closed with the Part A diagnostic that gates entry to Part B.
modules.tooltip: Week 8 (first half) . 2 hrs . Builds on P1-P5 - a body of Python work worth committing

topics.title: Unit 6.1 - Version Control Basics
topics.tooltip: What Git is and why it matters, the basic loop, GitHub Classroom, working via the web interface, good habits

topics.title: Unit 6.2 - Portfolio & Diagnostic
topics.tooltip: Personal portfolio repository, end-of-Part-A diagnostic
